ddls:
CREATE TABLE "components" (
  "component_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each component */,
  "component_type" TEXT /* Type of the component (e.g., Oscillator, Capacitor) */,
  "model_number" TEXT /* Model number of the component */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the component */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the component */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the component was created in the system */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the component was last updated in the system */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the component specification */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the component (e.g., active, deprecated, under review) */,
  "data_sheet_url" TEXT /* URL to the component's data sheet */,
  "last_reviewed_at" TEXT /* Date and time the component was last reviewed */,
  "approved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who approved the component */,
  "approved_at" TEXT /* Date and time the component was approved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "component_parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component the parameter belongs to */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "condition" TEXT /* Condition under which the parameter is measured */,
  "minimum_value" TEXT /* Minimum value of the parameter */,
  "typical_value" TEXT /* Typical value of the parameter */,
  "maximum_value" TEXT /* Maximum value of the parameter */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the parameter */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the parameter specification */,
  "is_custom" INTEGER /* Indicates if the parameter is custom or standard */,
  "last_updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the parameter */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the parameter was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_component_parameters_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., design engineer, quality assurance, product manager) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Specific permissions associated with the user */,
  "last_login_at" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "component_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the component */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the component was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access occurred */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for the access event */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Session ID for the access event */,
  "duration_seconds" INTEGER /* Duration of the access event in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_component_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_component_access_logs_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id")
)
